title: LuminCore Co-Founder Steps Down Ahead of Volta Deal Close
body:
Pieter van der Berg, one of LuminCore's two co-founders, is leaving the company before the anticipated close of its acquisition by Volta Systems, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

Van der Berg, who served as LuminCore's chief executive since its founding in 2021, informed employees last week of his intention to depart once the transaction closes. The other co-founder, Marta Hoekstra, is expected to remain and lead the photonics team inside Volta.

The departure is not unusual — acquisition-related founder exits are common — but the timing, ahead of regulatory clearance, raised some eyebrows internally. The European Commission has initiated a preliminary review of the deal, though no formal investigation has been announced.

Volta declined to comment on personnel matters. A person close to van der Berg described the decision as personal, saying he had been planning to take time off regardless of the acquisition outcome.

LuminCore had raised about $40 million across two rounds before agreeing to be acquired for $180 million in cash and stock. The Eindhoven-based company makes photonic interconnect components used in high-performance quantum and classical computing systems.
